query: Our onboarding README for the feature store is getting feedback like 'way too technical' from PMs and 'not technical enough, I had to read the source' from new engineers — in the same Slack thread, same week. We tried splitting the difference and it got worse. How do we fix it?
expected_behavior: Diagnoses the failure as a single-voice document being asked to serve two audiences with opposite depth needs, NOT as a tone or length problem.
Refuses the 'split the difference' / 'find a middle voice' approach and explains why it makes the doc worse for both audiences.
Recommends restructuring into per-audience sections (engineer section + PM section) with depth ceilings per audience, plus a shared TL;DR and a shared glossary.
